D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Astigmatism — Sagittal vs Tangential Focal Lines

Off-axis bundles have different focal lengths in the meridional and sagittal planes; sharp focus is replaced by two perpendicular focal lines with a "circle of least confusion" between

In human eyes: corneal asymmetry causes astigmatism; corrected by cylindrical-lens prescription. In camera lenses: anastigmat designs (1890s+) eliminate it. The minimum-blur position is between the two foci — that's where you focus when astigmatism is uncorrected.

Slide image-plane position through the two foci.
